Name: kv_cache_layout_optimization
Title: Optimizing KV-Cache Layouts for Efficient Transformer Inference on GPUs
Short Hypothesis: Redesigning the KV-cache layout to minimize memory bandwidth utilization and maximize tensor-core occupancy can significantly improve transformer inference latency and throughput on GPUs.
Related Work: Existing work on transformer inference optimization focuses on quantization, sparsity, and layer-skipping. However, there is limited research on optimizing the KV-cache layout specifically for GPU hardware. Recent papers on memory-efficient transformers and attention mechanisms provide foundational insights but do not address the hardware-specific layout optimizations proposed here.
Abstract: Transformer models have revolutionized NLP, but their inference efficiency remains a challenge, particularly on GPUs. This paper proposes a novel approach to optimizing the KV-cache layout to minimize memory bandwidth utilization and maximize tensor-core occupancy. By redesigning the KV-cache layout, we aim to reduce latency and increase throughput for transformer inference. Our method involves profiling memory access patterns, optimizing data placement, and leveraging CUDA/Triton kernels for efficient execution. We validate our approach through extensive hardware-faithful evaluation, including nvprof/Nsight traces, and demonstrate significant improvements in wall-clock latency and tokens-per-second on real hardware.
Experiments: Profile memory access patterns and bandwidth utilization for different KV-cache layouts using nvprof/Nsight.
Design and implement optimized KV-cache layouts leveraging CUDA/Triton kernels.
Compare wall-clock latency, tokens-per-second, and energy per token for baseline and optimized layouts on a commodity GPU.
Evaluate the impact of batch size and sequence length on performance gains.
Measure tensor-core occupancy and warp stall reasons to attribute performance improvements to specific hardware causes.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed optimizations may be highly specific to certain GPU architectures, limiting generalizability.
Significant engineering effort may be required to implement and tune the optimized kernels.
The benefits of the optimized layout may diminish for models with different attention mechanisms or architectures.
The approach may not yield significant improvements if memory bandwidth is not the bottleneck in the inference pipeline.
